projects.name: Pull Starboard Propeller & Shaft
projects.notes:
I have put this forward to Todd at Pacific Marine Center as a working outline of expenses for the worst case scenario. Hopefully we can pull the propeller and shaft, repair the shaft and re-install. 
I am waiting for a response from him. Sounds like he went in for knee surgery this week. 

WORST CASE SCENARIO
4-6 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
3-5 thousand for a shaft if needed. 
6-8 hours for replacing a cutlass bearing. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

    24hrs labor @ $145/hr = $4,000 with tax, 
   $6,000 materials with tax.
Estimate of safe worst case scenario ~$10,000: 

GUNING FOR 
4-8 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
$900 for shaft repair. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

  14 hours labor @ 145/hr = $2,000
  Shaft repair $900
$3,200 with tax

scopeTasks.desc:
GUNING FOR 
4-8 hours to pull the propeller and the shaft, the rudders don't interfere with the shaft removal. 
$1,000 for shaft repair. 
4-6 hours for installing new shaft and propeller. 

  14 hours labor @ 145/hr = $2,000
  Shaft repair $1,000
$3,200 with tax

projects.name: Fuel Inlet Panels
projects.notes:
Clean corrosion epoxy barrier coat then paint
*CHANGE OF SCOPE REQUEST IS THE SUBCONTRACTOR COST NOT QUOTED IN THE INITIAL ESTIMATE 
  $950
*Stripping the fuel inlet panels required an acid dip for each panel, scraping and then vapor honing. The estimate did not include any subcontractor cost as at the time it was submitted. It was thought that the normal vapor honing once would get the panels stripped of coatings.

scopeTasks.desc: Clean corrosion.  Epoxy barrier coat then paint with color matched enamel.

Vapor Hone
Acid dip and scrape.
Vapor Hone second time

projects.name: Buff/Wax During Haul Out
projects.notes:
Buff/wax 
1. Below the lower rub rail. 
2. Forward of the lower rub rail I suggest buffing waxing from the waterline up to the halfway mark between the waterline and the cap rail.  This will leave the rest of the bow and topsides accessible when she is back in the water.

scopeTasks.desc: Wax Hull Below The Rub Rails

Fine Compound, 52.50

projects.name: Exterior Varnish Repairs & Refinish
projects.notes: This had been discussed yet it hadn't appeared on any Worklists yet.

scopeTasks.desc: Russ pulled the canvas cap rail covers and gave me an estimate on varnish repairs. This includes varnish on the port rail (approximately 10 coats of varnish)

tasks.desc: I spoke with the varnish pro regarding scheduling 4/2 and it looks like it makes sense to do all the varnish work while Undaunted is on the dock in Blaine. That adds travel time to Blaine in the amount of 1 hour per 7 hours of work to be done. 45 hours is approx 7 days of work. 7 days of work is 7 hours of travel time to and from Blaine.

projects.name: Oil Pressure Gauges Alarming
projects.notes:
Functional to AK and back through the Fall. 
New sending units will be tested on her trip to Anacortes for haul out. My sense is that the present location of these exposes them to too much heat. If the new sending units resolve the issue as expected we can relocate the sending units where they will not be exposed to the heat exposure right there next to the engine block.

**Will follow up with Change of Scope when we are clear that the new sending units work.

scopeTasks.desc:
When the starboard main engine is heat soaked and at low idle we are getting 13psi of oil pressure with the mechanical gauge I installed Monday. At the same time the VDO gauge shows well under 10psi and the alarm is sounding.
Consult with Tri County for information and propose next steps.
